title: Marbella No Speedboat Hire Licence: Costs, Tips & 2026 Guide
html:
If you are planning a speedboat hire in Marbella No, the good news is that you do not need a licence to rent a small motorboat, provided you stick to local rules. Departing from Puerto José Banús, you can expect to pay from around €350 for a half-day rental. BoatHire24 makes it straightforward to compare options.

Do you need a licence to rent a speedboat in Marbella No?

No licence is required to rent a small powerboat in Marbella No, but you must be over 18 and present a valid ID or passport. Most boats up to 15 horsepower or a certain length can be hired without a Spanish boating licence. For larger vessels, the operator will confirm exact requirements at the time of booking. In 2026, the same rules apply across the Costa del Sol.

Where to set off in Marbella No

Marbella No is served by several excellent marinas that act as departure points for speedboat hire. Puerto José Banús is the most famous, with its luxury port and easy access to the open sea. Puerto Deportivo de Marbella, located near the old town, offers a more local atmosphere. Other options include Puerto Cabopino, a smaller marina to the west, and Puerto de la Bajadilla, a working fishing port. Each provides direct access to the Mediterranean and nearby anchorages such as Playa de Levante and the coast towards Estepona.

How much does it cost to rent a speedboat in Marbella No?

Renting a speedboat in Marbella No typically costs between €350 and €800 for a half-day, depending on the size and engine power. Prices vary by season, with peak months from June to September commanding higher rates. A shared tour might cost as little as €80 per person. See the price breakdown below.

  • Shared tour (RIB or small speedboat, 2 hours): from €80 per person
  • Small motorboat half-day (up to 20 ft, self-drive): from €350
  • Catamaran full-day (crewed, for up to 12 guests): from €1,200
  • Crewed yacht (30 ft or more, full day): from €1,600

Which boat to rent

Marbella No has a wide choice of vessels, from nimble runabouts to offshore cruisers. You can book a charter online to see the full range. Here are four popular options.

  1. QuickSilver Activ 605 Open, ideal for a family day trip, seats up to 6, from €450 half-day. See this speedboat for details.
  2. Axopar 22, a sporty cabin cruiser for 8, from €550 half-day.
  3. Azimut 42, a luxury flybridge for 10, from €1,200 half-day.
  4. Lagoon 40, a catamaran for 12, from €1,500 full day.

When is the best time to hire a boat in Marbella No?

The peak season runs from June to September, when the weather is hottest and the sea is calmest. The prevailing Poniente wind can bring afternoon breezes, which are ideal for sailing. Spring and autumn, from April to May and October to November, offer pleasant conditions and lower charter rates. Winter rentals are less common. BoatHire24 provides listings year-round.

Book early for July and August: popular models like the QuickSilver Activ 605 Open are often reserved weeks ahead. A morning departure gives you flat water and quiet anchorages.

At a glance

  • Price band: €350, €1,600 (half-day to full-day)
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Trip length: 2 hours to full day
  • Capacity: up to 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Marbella No

Can you rent a speedboat without a licence in Marbella No?

Yes, for boats that fall under the Spanish regulations for unlicensed rental, you can hire a speedboat without a licence provided you meet the age and identity requirements set by the operator.

What is the minimum age to drive a speedboat in Marbella No?

You must be at least 18 years old to drive a rented speedboat in Marbella No, and some operators may ask for a short on-water briefing before departure.

Is it safe to rent a speedboat in Marbella No for the first time?

Yes, as long as you follow the safety briefing and stick to sheltered coastal waters; the marinas around Marbella No are well-maintained and the sea conditions are generally mild in summer.

How far can you go in a half-day rental from Marbella No?

A half-day rental typically covers up to 15 nautical miles along the coast, giving you time to reach places like Puerto Cabopino or the bay of Estepona and back.

To find the perfect boat for your trip, browse the listings on BoatHire24. If you own a boat in Marbella No, you can list your vessel and earn extra income by reaching thousands of travellers.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Marbella No and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
